rookie.title: Ghostly Debts
rookie.flavor: The jukebox still plays Bon Jovi, but Sal Benedetto will never hear it again. In a city where everyone owes someone, some debts get collected in blood.
rookie.emptyState: The last slice went cold hours ago.

items.detail: Local loan shark with expensive tastes

items.detail: Night shift waitress struggling with bills

items.name: Storage Room
items.detail: Dark basement filled with supplies

clues.heading: Bloody Apron
clues.text: Frank Kowalski's maintenance apron hangs in the Storage Room, splattered with fresh blood.
clues.alone: Frank's apron is covered in blood.

clues.heading: Heated Argument
clues.text: Customer heard Tony Carlucci screaming at Sal about overdue payments around 9 PM.
clues.alone: Tony and Sal had a violent argument.

clues.heading: Hidden Cash
clues.text: Maria Santos had $500 cash tucked in her purse, unusual for someone behind on rent.
clues.alone: Maria was carrying suspicious amounts of cash.

clues.heading: Murder Weapon
clues.text: The Meat Tenderizer from the Kitchen shows blood and hair, found near the Back Office.
clues.alone: The meat tenderizer was used to kill Sal.

clues.heading: Work Schedule
clues.text: Frank Kowalski was scheduled to fix the freezer that night, explaining his bloody apron from handling raw meat.
clues.alone: Frank's blood came from maintenance work on the freezer.

links.insight: Frank's bloody apron came from legitimate maintenance work, not murder.

clues.heading: Collection Route
clues.text: Tony Carlucci collected his money from Sal and left peacefully, witnessed by three customers.
clues.alone: Tony got his money and left without violence.

links.insight: Tony's argument was resolved when Sal paid his debt in full.

clues.heading: Bank Records
clues.text: Maria Santos received her first paycheck in weeks that day, accounting for the cash.
clues.alone: Maria's cash came from her legitimate paycheck.

links.insight: Maria's cash was her legitimate paycheck, not stolen money from Sal.

clues.heading: Office Sounds
clues.text: Cook heard Sal arguing with someone in the Back Office around 10 PM, after Tony left.
clues.alone: Someone else confronted Sal after Tony's departure.

links.insight: Sal was killed in the Back Office by someone who came after Tony left.

rookie.epilogue: Frank Kowalski had borrowed money from Sal months ago to pay for his mother's medical bills. When Sal demanded immediate repayment with crushing interest, Frank snapped. He grabbed the Meat Tenderizer from the Kitchen and confronted Sal in the Back Office, striking him down in desperation. The quiet maintenance worker's gentle nature couldn't survive the pressure of impossible debt.

clues.heading: Bloody Apron
clues.text: Frank Kowalski's maintenance apron hangs in the Storage Room, splattered with fresh blood.
clues.alone: Frank's apron is covered in blood.

clues.heading: Witness Account
clues.text: Reliable customer swears they saw Joey Benedetto leaving through the back door at 11:15 PM.
clues.alone: Joey was seen leaving after the murder occurred.

clues.heading: Time Punch
clues.text: Frank Kowalski's timecard shows he clocked out at exactly 10:45 PM that night.
clues.alone: Frank officially left before the murder window.

clues.heading: Murder Weapon
clues.text: The Meat Tenderizer from the Kitchen shows blood and hair, found near the Back Office.
clues.alone: The meat tenderizer was used to kill Sal.

clues.heading: Freezer Repair
clues.text: Frank Kowalski was fixing the broken freezer that evening, explaining the blood from handling raw meat.
clues.alone: Frank's blood came from legitimate repair work.

links.insight: Frank's bloody apron came from legitimate maintenance work, not murder.

clues.heading: Security Camera
clues.text: Parking lot camera shows Joey Benedetto arriving at 11:20 PM, five minutes after the witness claimed to see him leave.
clues.alone: Joey actually arrived after he was supposedly seen leaving.

links.insight: The witness mistook someone else for Joey, who actually arrived after the murder.

clues.heading: Hidden Key
clues.text: Frank Kowalski kept a spare key to return for overtime work during the 10:00-11:00 PM window.
clues.alone: Frank could return during the murder timeframe.

links.insight: Frank clocked out but could return with his spare key during the 10:00-11:00 PM murder window.

clues.heading: Final Argument
clues.text: Maria Santos heard Sal arguing with someone about money in the Back Office around 10:30 PM.
clues.alone: Sal was killed during a confrontation about money.

links.insight: Sal was killed in the Back Office during the 10:00-11:00 PM window with the meat tenderizer.

detective.epilogue: Frank Kowalski had borrowed money from Sal months ago to pay for his mother's medical bills. When Sal demanded immediate repayment with crushing interest, Frank returned after hours using his spare key. He grabbed the Meat Tenderizer from the Kitchen and confronted Sal in the Back Office during the 10:00-11:00 PM window, striking him down in desperation. The quiet maintenance worker's gentle nature couldn't survive the pressure of impossible debt.

clues.heading: Bloody Apron
clues.text: Frank Kowalski's maintenance apron hangs in the Storage Room, splattered with fresh blood.
clues.alone: Frank's apron is covered in blood.

clues.heading: Eyewitness Report
clues.text: Credible witness swears Detective Ray Murphy was responding to a domestic disturbance across town all night.
clues.alone: Ray Murphy has a solid alibi from police dispatch.

clues.heading: Missing Key
clues.text: Frank Kowalski's Spare Key to the building was found inside Sal's Back Office desk drawer.
clues.alone: Frank's spare key was inside the locked office.

clues.heading: Murder Weapon
clues.text: The Meat Tenderizer from the Kitchen shows blood and hair, found near the Back Office.
clues.alone: The meat tenderizer was used to kill Sal.

clues.heading: Freezer Repair
clues.text: Frank Kowalski was fixing the broken freezer that evening, explaining the blood from handling raw meat.
clues.alone: Frank's blood came from legitimate repair work.

links.insight: Frank's bloody apron came from legitimate maintenance work, not murder.

clues.heading: Phone Records
clues.text: Detective Ray Murphy called in sick to dispatch, no domestic disturbance call existed that night.
clues.alone: Ray Murphy's alibi was completely fabricated.

links.insight: Ray Murphy lied about his whereabouts and had no legitimate police alibi.

clues.heading: Broken Entry
clues.text: Someone forced the Back Door lock mechanism, leaving metal shavings and fresh scratch marks.
clues.alone: The killer broke in through the back door.

links.insight: Frank's key was inside because the killer broke in through the Back Door, not using a key.

clues.heading: Secret Partnership
clues.text: Maria Santos was Detective Ray Murphy's confidential informant, helping him track Sal's loan shark operations.
clues.alone: Maria was working with Detective Murphy against Sal.

links.insight: Detective Murphy killed Sal in the Back Office with the meat tenderizer, using Maria as his inside source.

inspector.epilogue: Detective Ray Murphy had been investigating Sal's loan shark operation while secretly owing him thousands in gambling debts. Using Maria Santos as his confidential informant, Murphy learned Sal's nightly routine. He broke in through the Back Door during the 10:00-11:00 PM window, grabbed the Meat Tenderizer from the Kitchen, and confronted Sal in the Back Office. When Sal threatened to expose Murphy's gambling addiction to Internal Affairs, the corrupt cop struck him down, betraying both his badge and his informant's trust.
